summary: The manuscript reads cleanly as a theorem-first note and it keeps the no-attack boundary intact, but the reader-facing audit trail is weakened by source-path references that do not resolve in the current checkout and by a supporting section that still occupies more narrative weight than it needs.
strengths: The abstract, introduction, and conclusion consistently state that no forgery attack is constructed.
The main reduction theorem and the GAP 7 / GAP 10 story are easy to identify on a first read.

findings.summary: The manuscript's formal-source path references do not match the current repository layout.
findings.rationale: A reader cannot follow the paper's theorem-to-source audit trail when the prose cites formal/proof-gap-analysis/... paths but the checkout only contains artifacts/aristotle-proof-gap-analysis/.
evidence_refs: paper/main.tex#The Reduction and Its Lean Encoding
findings.required_action: Either update the manuscript and support notes to the actual artifacts/... paths, or explicitly document the alias mapping in the manuscript itself rather than only in the review-support manifest.

findings.summary: The supporting surrogate-model section is already disciplined, but it still deserves additional compression so the formal-verification contribution dominates the page budget.
findings.rationale: The paper's strongest material is the reduction and gap taxonomy; the supporting surrogate section is not wrong, but a shorter treatment would sharpen the paper for expert readers.
findings.required_action: Compress the surrogate-model section further or explicitly mark it as appendix material in the manuscript outline.
